claim: Koan.Scheduling can be cut: its only two real consumers (S5.Recs S5BootstrapTask, Koan.Service.KoanContext JobMaintenanceTask) use only the IOnStartup+IHasTimeout subset and can migrate to JOBS-0005 [JobAction(Schedule = "@boot", Timeout = ...)]; Koan.Web's /.well-known/scheduling endpoint can re-point at Jobs schedule state or be dropped; OPS-0050 gets marked superseded by JOBS-0005.
corrections: The 'ReadinessGate' semantic concern is moot: it was never implemented. SchedulingOptions.ReadinessGate is a dead config placeholder (TECHNICAL.md:25 'future use; placeholder wired in options'); SchedulingOrchestrator never reads it, and IOnStartup tasks are fire-and-forget (`_ = r.RunOnce(stoppingToken)`), so nothing blocks traffic today. There is no run-before-traffic semantic to preserve — the migration loses nothing.

OPS-0050 is not merely 'superseded' — it is ~60% unimplemented. ICronScheduled, IProvidesLock, IAllowedWindows, IHealthFacts are declared interfaces the orchestrator ignores ('cron reserved for Phase 2', SchedulingOrchestrator.cs:65); Phase-3 BootstrapJobRunner/IBootstrapTask don't exist anywhere in src. Mark it 'Superseded by JOBS-0005; Phases 2-3 never implemented'.

@boot semantic delta (favorable): IOnStartup ran in-process on every node every boot; @boot submits one job per booting node's worker start (JobWorkerService.cs:48) against the per-type singleton work-item '__koan_job_singleton__' (JobCoordinator.cs:12,101), each submission executed exactly once cluster-wide via the §20 CAS claim — possibly on a different node than the booting one. Add [JobIdempotent] to coalesce concurrent multi-node boots; both consumers' work is idempotent against shared stores, so this is equal-or-stronger.

Hidden prod behavior change: Scheduling auto-disables outside Development unless a Koan:Scheduling section exists (Scheduling KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:24-28), and NO appsettings in the entire repo configures it — both tasks silently never ran in Production. Jobs' worker defaults on (EnableWorker=true, Mode=Normal, JobsOptions.cs:16,50), so post-migration they WILL run in prod. Desirable (especially for JobMaintenanceTask) but must be stated in the migration note.

Retry delta: Jobs retries failed actions (DefaultMaxAttempts=3, JobsOptions.cs:28); Scheduling never retried a startup task until next boot. JobMaintenanceTask swallows its own exceptions (no change); S5BootstrapTask should set MaxAttempts=1 to preserve exact one-shot semantics, or accept retries as an improvement.

Drop the endpoint, don't re-point: /.well-known/Koan/scheduling has zero code consumers (only non-Koan.Web appearance is S7.Meridian's generated swagger JSON; the archived S2 client fetches /observability, not /scheduling), and it currently advertises the dead readinessGate option (WellKnownController.cs:98). Re-pointing would mint new API surface for nobody. Note Koan.Web.csproj:20 hard-references Koan.Scheduling, so today EVERY web app hosts SchedulingOrchestrator's 1-second poll loop even with zero tasks — the cut de-bloats every app's boot.

Cut scope is larger than the claim states: Koan.Core carries Scheduling residue to clean up — KoanServiceActions.Scheduling (TriggerTask/ListTasks, KoanServiceActions.cs:24-28), KoanServiceEvents.Scheduling (TaskExecuted/TaskFailed/TaskTimeout, KoanServiceEvents.cs:25-29), and IScheduledTask doc-comment examples in ServiceCollectionScanExtensions.cs:43 and NotAutoRegisteredAttribute.cs:5,12. Plus a docs sweep (modules-overview, module-ledger, capability-map, bootstrap-lifecycle, provenance-configuration-descriptors, samples/CATALOG.md, DX-0045/0046, BACKUP-SYSTEM.md) and Koan.sln removal. Zero test projects reference Koan.Scheduling, so the cut costs no coverage.

Migration mechanics the claim skips: IKoanJob<TSelf> requires the task to become an Entity<TSelf> work-item — the singleton is Saved on every trigger (JobCoordinator.cs:48), so on the durable tier a row lands in the app's data store; use [JobPersistence(JobPersistence.InMemory)] if that's unwanted. Both projects must swap their Koan.Scheduling ProjectReference (S5.Recs.csproj:27, Koan.Service.KoanContext.csproj:39) for Koan.Jobs (neither references it today) and delete the manual IScheduledTask registrations (S5BootstrapTaskRegistration.cs:10; KoanContext Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:61). DI is preserved via ctx.Services (JobContext.cs:44).
evidence: src/Koan.Jobs/JobScheduler.cs:118 — ParseSchedule recognizes '@boot' (ScheduleKind.Boot); :43-50 SubmitBootActionsAsync triggers each @boot action once at startup
src/Koan.Jobs/JobWorkerService.cs:47-48 — boot sequence: RecoverAsync then SubmitBootActionsAsync, once per process start; :39 disabled only in Inline mode / EnableWorker=false

src/Koan.Jobs/JobOrchestrator.cs:114,136 — policy.Timeout enforced via linked CTS; timeout settles as failure (TimeoutException)

src/Koan.Jobs/JobCoordinator.cs:12,98-103 — TriggerAsync loads/creates per-type singleton work-item '__koan_job_singleton__' and submits; :48 binding.Save persists the work-item; :52-57 coalesce only with [JobIdempotent] and only against active jobs
src/Koan.Jobs/JobContext.cs:44 — public IServiceProvider Services (DI access for static Execute handlers)

src/Koan.Scheduling/SchedulingOrchestrator.cs:61-63 — startup tasks fired as `_ = r.RunOnce(stoppingToken)` (fire-and-forget; nothing blocks readiness/traffic); :65 'cron reserved for Phase 2'; :66-74 1-second poll loop runs regardless of task count

src/Koan.Scheduling/SchedulingOptions.cs:6 + TECHNICAL.md:25 — ReadinessGate=true is a dead placeholder ('future use'); never read by the orchestrator (grep: only options/provenance/registrar references)

src/Koan.Scheduling/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:24-28 — Scheduling force-disabled outside Development unless Koan:Scheduling section exists; repo-wide grep of appsettings*.json finds NO Koan:Scheduling config — both consumers' tasks never ran in Production

samples/S5.Recs/Tasks/S5BootstrapTask.cs:9 + src/Services/code-intelligence/Koan.Service.KoanContext/Tasks/IndexingJobMaintenanceTask.cs:21 — the ONLY two IScheduledTask implementors repo-wide (repo grep), both exactly `IScheduledTask, IOnStartup, IHasTimeout`, both idempotent (S5 checks stats before seeding; JobMaintenanceTask is cleanup over the app-domain Job entity, not Koan.Jobs JobRecord)
samples/S5.Recs/Tasks/S5BootstrapTaskRegistration.cs:10 + src/Services/code-intelligence/Koan.Service.KoanContext/Initialization/KoanAutoRegistrar.cs:61 — the two manual registrations to delete

samples/S5.Recs/S5.Recs.csproj:27 + Koan.Service.KoanContext.csproj:39 — both reference Koan.Scheduling; NEITHER references Koan.Jobs today (Koan.Jobs referenced only by S14.AdapterBench, Jobs.Transport.Messaging, Jobs TestKit, Integration.Bootstrap tests)

src/Koan.Web/Koan.Web.csproj:20 — Koan.Web hard-references Koan.Scheduling (every web app transitively hosts the orchestrator); src/Koan.Web/Controllers/WellKnownController.cs:74-105 — the endpoint renders scheduling:task:* health facts + the dead readinessGate option; repo-wide grep shows no client/sample code fetches /.well-known/Koan/scheduling (only S7.Meridian's generated meridian-swagger.json:2798 lists it; archived S2 client fetches /observability)

docs/decisions/OPS-0050-scheduling-and-bootstrap-unification.md:27-36,57-61 — promised readiness gating (item 4), cron/locks/windows (Phase 2), BootstrapJobRunner/IBootstrapTask (Phase 3): none implemented (grep finds no BootstrapJobRunner/IBootstrapTask in src)
tests/ — zero files reference Scheduling or IScheduledTask (grep): the module has no test coverage to lose
refinedRecommendation: Cut Koan.Scheduling (16 files, 2 consumers, 0 tests, ADR ~60% unimplemented). Recipe: (1) S5.Recs — replace S5BootstrapTask with `[JobAction("bootstrap", Schedule = "@boot", Timeout = "00:05:00", MaxAttempts = 1)] [JobIdempotent] sealed class S5Bootstrap : Entity<S5Bootstrap>, IKoanJob<S5Bootstrap>` whose static Execute resolves ISeedService via ctx.Services; delete S5BootstrapTaskRegistration; swap csproj reference Koan.Scheduling→Koan.Jobs. (2) KoanContext — same shape with `[JobAction("maintain", Schedule = "@boot", Timeout = "00:02:00")] [JobIdempotent]`; optionally add a real recurring cadence (e.g. Schedule = "1.00:00:00") — Jobs delivers the recurring maintenance Scheduling's unimplemented cron never could; use [JobPersistence(JobPersistence.InMemory)] if singleton rows in the context store are unwanted; delete the IScheduledTask registration line. (3) Koan.Web — DROP the /.well-known/Koan/scheduling action + route constant + the csproj reference (zero consumers exist; do NOT re-point — that mints new surface for nobody; Jobs state is already queryable via MyJob.Jobs.Where/JobMetric.Summary if a surface is ever wanted). (4) Koan.Core — remove KoanServiceActions.Scheduling + KoanServiceEvents.Scheduling groups and re-target the IScheduledTask doc-comment examples. (5) Delete src/Koan.Scheduling, remove from Koan.sln, sweep docs (modules-overview, module-ledger, capability-map, bootstrap-lifecycle, provenance docs, CATALOG.md, DX-0045/0046, BACKUP-SYSTEM.md). (6) Mark OPS-0050 'Superseded by JOBS-0005', noting readiness gating, cron, locks, windows, and the bootstrap runner were never implemented, and that Jobs provides the durable equivalents (per-action Timeout enforced; @boot fires per node boot, executes once cluster-wide per submission via CAS claim; [JobIdempotent] coalesces concurrent boots). State two deliberate behavior changes in the commit/ADR note: (a) the tasks now run in Production (Scheduling silently disabled itself outside Development and no appsettings ever enabled it — the old tasks were dev-only in practice); (b) failures now retry per DefaultMaxAttempts unless MaxAttempts=1 is pinned. Net semantics: nothing blocks traffic before or after (Scheduling's startup tasks were always fire-and-forget; ReadinessGate was a dead option), so no run-before-traffic guarantee is lost.
